title: Catevala × subrigida
intro: Catevala × subrigida, also known as Cat's Claw, is a woody vine native to the Amazon rainforest, with its thorns resembling a cat's claw. The health benefits of Cat's Claw include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cat's Claw include alkaloids, such as isoflavonoids and oxindole alkaloids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Cat's Claw include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support joint health and immune function.
taxonomy: Catevala × subrigida is a hybrid of the genus Catevala, which belongs to the family Ranunculaceae and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The genus Catevala is further divided into several species, including Catevala subrigida. Catevala × subrigida is a cross between two species of the Catevala genus. Due to its hybrid nature, the exact classification of Catevala × subrigida can be complex and may vary depending on the specific cross. However, it is generally classified as a member of the Ranunculaceae family.
